registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

policyText.description: The contents of the registry permissions policy that was deleted.

registryId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID associated with the registry.

rules.description: A list of signing rules. Each rule defines a signing profile and optional repository filters that determine which images are automatically signed. Maximum of 10 rules.

pullThroughCacheRules.description: The details of the pull through cache rules.

nextToken.description: The nextToken value to include in a future DescribePullThroughCacheRulesRequest request. When the results of a DescribePullThroughCacheRulesRequest request exceed maxResults, this value can be used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

ecrRepositoryPrefix.description: The Amazon ECR repository prefix associated with the pull through cache rule.

upstreamRegistryUrl.description: The upstream registry URL associated with the pull through cache rule.

createdAt.description: The date and time the pull through cache was created.

registryId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID associated with the registry the pull through cache rule is associated with.

credentialArn.description: The ARN of the Secrets Manager secret associated with the pull through cache rule.

customRoleArn.description: The ARN of the IAM role associated with the pull through cache rule.

upstreamRepositoryPrefix.description: The upstream repository prefix associated with the pull through cache rule.

upstreamRegistry.description: The name of the upstream source registry associated with the pull through cache rule.

updatedAt.description: The date and time, in JavaScript date format, when the pull through cache rule was last updated.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

rules.description: An array of objects representing the replication destinations and repository filters for a replication configuration.

repositories.description: A list of repository objects corresponding to valid repositories.

nextToken.description: The nextToken value to include in a future DescribeRepositories request. When the results of a DescribeRepositories request exceed maxResults, this value can be used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

repositoryArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that identifies the repository. The ARN contains the arn:aws:ecr namespace, followed by the region of the repository, Amazon Web Services account ID of the repository owner, repository namespace, and repository name. For example, arn:aws:ecr:region:012345678910:repository-namespace/repository-name.

registryId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID associated with the registry that contains the repository.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository.

repositoryUri.description: The URI for the repository. You can use this URI for container image push and pull operations.

createdAt.description: The date and time, in JavaScript date format, when the repository was created.

imageTagMutability.description: The tag mutability setting for the repository.

imageTagMutabilityExclusionFilters.description: A list of filters that specify which image tags are excluded from the repository's image tag mutability setting.

imageScanningConfiguration.description: The image scanning configuration for a repository.

scanOnPush.description: The setting that determines whether images are scanned after being pushed to a repository. If set to true, images will be scanned after being pushed. If this parameter is not specified, it will default to false and images will not be scanned unless a scan is manually started with the API_StartImageScan API.

encryptionConfiguration.description: The encryption configuration for the repository. This determines how the contents of your repository are encrypted at rest.

encryptionType.description:
The encryption type to use.

If you use the KMS encryption type, the contents of the repository will be encrypted using server-side encryption with Key Management Service key stored in KMS. When you use KMS to encrypt your data, you can either use the default Amazon Web Services managed KMS key for Amazon ECR, or specify your own KMS key, which you already created.

If you use the KMS_DSSE encryption type, the contents of the repository will be encrypted with two layers of encryption using server-side encryption with the KMS Management Service key stored in KMS. Similar to the KMS encryption type, you can either use the default Amazon Web Services managed KMS key for Amazon ECR, or specify your own KMS key, which you've already created.

If you use the AES256 encryption type, Amazon ECR uses server-side encryption with Amazon S3-managed encryption keys which encrypts the images in the repository using an AES256 encryption algorithm.

For more information, see Amazon ECR encryption at rest in the Amazon Elastic Container Registry User Guide.

kmsKey.description: If you use the KMS encryption type, specify the KMS key to use for encryption. The alias, key ID, or full ARN of the KMS key can be specified. The key must exist in the same Region as the repository. If no key is specified, the default Amazon Web Services managed KMS key for Amazon ECR will be used.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryCreationTemplates.description: The details of the repository creation templates.

nextToken.description: The nextToken value to include in a future DescribeRepositoryCreationTemplates request. When the results of a DescribeRepositoryCreationTemplates request exceed maxResults, this value can be used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

prefix.description: The repository namespace prefix associated with the repository creation template.

description.description: The description associated with the repository creation template.

encryptionConfiguration.description: The encryption configuration associated with the repository creation template.

encryptionType.description:
The encryption type to use.

If you use the KMS encryption type, the contents of the repository will be encrypted using server-side encryption with Key Management Service key stored in KMS. When you use KMS to encrypt your data, you can either use the default Amazon Web Services managed KMS key for Amazon ECR, or specify your own KMS key, which you already created. For more information, see Protecting data using server-side encryption with an KMS key stored in Key Management Service (SSE-KMS) in the Amazon Simple Storage Service Console Developer Guide.

If you use the AES256 encryption type, Amazon ECR uses server-side encryption with Amazon S3-managed encryption keys which encrypts the images in the repository using an AES256 encryption algorithm. For more information, see Protecting data using server-side encryption with Amazon S3-managed encryption keys (SSE-S3) in the Amazon Simple Storage Service Console Developer Guide.

kmsKey.description: If you use the KMS encryption type, specify the KMS key to use for encryption. The full ARN of the KMS key must be specified. The key must exist in the same Region as the repository. If no key is specified, the default Amazon Web Services managed KMS key for Amazon ECR will be used.

resourceTags.description: The metadata to apply to the repository to help you categorize and organize. Each tag consists of a key and an optional value, both of which you define. Tag keys can have a maximum character length of 128 characters, and tag values can have a maximum length of 256 characters.

imageTagMutability.description: The tag mutability setting for the repository. If this parameter is omitted, the default setting of MUTABLE will be used which will allow image tags to be overwritten. If IMMUTABLE is specified, all image tags within the repository will be immutable which will prevent them from being overwritten.

imageTagMutabilityExclusionFilters.description: A list of filters that specify which image tags are excluded from the repository creation template's image tag mutability setting.

repositoryPolicy.description: The repository policy to apply to repositories created using the template. A repository policy is a permissions policy associated with a repository to control access permissions.

lifecyclePolicy.description: The lifecycle policy to use for repositories created using the template.

appliedFor.description: A list of enumerable Strings representing the repository creation scenarios that this template will apply towards. The two supported scenarios are PULL_THROUGH_CACHE and REPLICATION

customRoleArn.description: The ARN of the role to be assumed by Amazon ECR. Amazon ECR will assume your supplied role when the customRoleArn is specified. When this field isn't specified, Amazon ECR will use the service-linked role for the repository creation template.

createdAt.description: The date and time, in JavaScript date format, when the repository creation template was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time, in JavaScript date format, when the repository creation template was last updated.

authorizationData.description:
A list of authorization token data objects that correspond to the registryIds values in the request.

The size of the authorization token returned by Amazon ECR is not fixed. We recommend that you don't make assumptions about the maximum size.

authorizationToken.description: A base64-encoded string that contains authorization data for the specified Amazon ECR registry. When the string is decoded, it is presented in the format user:password for private registry authentication using docker login.

expiresAt.description: The Unix time in seconds and milliseconds when the authorization token expires. Authorization tokens are valid for 12 hours.

proxyEndpoint.description: The registry URL to use for this authorization token in a docker login command. The Amazon ECR registry URL format is https://aws_account_id.dkr.ecr.region.amazonaws.com. For example, https://012345678910.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com..

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

policyText.description: The JSON text of the permissions policy for a registry.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

scanType.description: The type of scanning configured for the registry.

rules.description: The scanning rules associated with the registry.

registryId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID associated with the registry.

rules.description: A list of signing rules. Each rule defines a signing profile and optional repository filters that determine which images are automatically signed. Maximum of 10 rules.

pullTimeUpdateExclusions.description: The list of IAM principal ARNs that are excluded from having their image pull times recorded.

nextToken.description: The nextToken value to include in a future ListPullTimeUpdateExclusions request. When the results of a ListPullTimeUpdateExclusions request exceed maxResults, this value can be used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

scanType.description: The type of scanning configured for the registry.

rules.description: The scanning rules associated with the registry.

layers.description: A list of image layer objects corresponding to the image layer references in the request.

layerAvailability.description: The availability status of the image layer.

layerSize.description: The size, in bytes, of the image layer.

mediaType.description: The media type of the layer, such as application/vnd.docker.image.rootfs.diff.tar.gzip or application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip.

imageIds.description: The image IDs of the deleted images.

images.description: A list of image objects corresponding to the image references in the request.

registryId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID associated with the registry containing the image.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository associated with the image.

imageId.description: An object containing the image tag and image digest associated with an image.

imageManifest.description: The image manifest associated with the image.

imageManifestMediaType.description: The manifest media type of the image.

scanningConfigurations.description: The scanning configuration for the requested repositories.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository.

scanOnPush.description: Whether or not scan on push is configured for the repository.

appliedScanFilters.description: The scan filters applied to the repository.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

uploadId.description: The upload ID associated with the layer.

upstreamRegistryUrl.description: The upstream registry URL associated with the pull through cache rule.

updatedAt.description: The date and time, in JavaScript date format, when the repository creation template was last updated.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

policyText.description: The JSON repository policy that was deleted from the repository.

principalArn.description: The ARN of the IAM principal that was removed from the pull time update exclusion list.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

imageId.description: An object with identifying information for an image in an Amazon ECR repository.

replicationStatuses.description: The replication status details for the images in the specified repository.

region.description: The destination Region for the image replication.

registryId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID associated with the registry to which the image belongs.

failureCode.description: The failure code for a replication that has failed.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

imageId.description: An object with identifying information for an image in an Amazon ECR repository.

imageScanFindings.description: The information contained in the image scan findings.

nextToken.description: The nextToken value to include in a future DescribeImageScanFindings request. When the results of a DescribeImageScanFindings request exceed maxResults, this value can be used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository.

imageId.description: An object with identifying information for the image.

registryId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID associated with the registry.

signingStatuses.description: A list of signing statuses for the specified image. Each status corresponds to a signing profile.

signingProfileArn.description: The ARN of the Amazon Web Services Signer signing profile used to sign the image.

failureCode.description: The failure code, which is only present if status is FAILED.

failureReason.description: A description of why signing the image failed. This field is only present if status is FAILED.

status.description:
The image's signing status. Possible values are:

  • IN_PROGRESS - Signing is currently in progress.

  • COMPLETE - The signature was successfully generated.

  • FAILED - Signing failed. See failureCode and failureReason for details.

imageDetails.description: A list of ImageDetail objects that contain data about the image.

nextToken.description: The nextToken value to include in a future DescribeImages request. When the results of a DescribeImages request exceed maxResults, this value can be used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

registryId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID associated with the registry to which this image belongs.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository to which this image belongs.

imageTags.description: The list of tags associated with this image.

imageSizeInBytes.description:
The size, in bytes, of the image in the repository.

If the image is a manifest list, this will be the max size of all manifests in the list.

Starting with Docker version 1.9, the Docker client compresses image layers before pushing them to a V2 Docker registry. The output of the docker images command shows the uncompressed image size. Therefore, Docker might return a larger image than the image shown in the Amazon Web Services Management Console.

imagePushedAt.description: The date and time, expressed in standard JavaScript date format, at which the current image was pushed to the repository.

status.description: The current state of an image scan.

description.description: The description of the image scan status.

imageScanFindingsSummary.description: A summary of the last completed image scan.

imageScanCompletedAt.description: The time of the last completed image scan.

vulnerabilitySourceUpdatedAt.description: The time when the vulnerability data was last scanned.

findingSeverityCounts.description: The image vulnerability counts, sorted by severity.

imageManifestMediaType.description: The media type of the image manifest.

artifactMediaType.description: The artifact media type of the image.

lastRecordedPullTime.description:
The date and time, expressed in standard JavaScript date format, when Amazon ECR recorded the last image pull.

Amazon ECR refreshes the last image pull timestamp at least once every 24 hours. For example, if you pull an image once a day then the lastRecordedPullTime timestamp will indicate the exact time that the image was last pulled. However, if you pull an image once an hour, because Amazon ECR refreshes the lastRecordedPullTime timestamp at least once every 24 hours, the result may not be the exact time that the image was last pulled.

subjectManifestDigest.description: The digest of the subject manifest for images that are referrers.

lastArchivedAt.description: The date and time, expressed in standard JavaScript date format, when the image was last transitioned to Amazon ECR archive.

lastActivatedAt.description: The date and time, expressed in standard JavaScript date format, when the image was last restored from Amazon ECR archive to Amazon ECR standard.

name.description: Retrieves the name of the account setting.

value.description: The setting value for the setting name. The following are valid values for the basic scan type being used: AWS_NATIVE or CLAIR. The following are valid values for the registry policy scope being used: V1 or V2.

downloadUrl.description: The pre-signed Amazon S3 download URL for the requested layer.

layerDigest.description: The digest of the image layer to download.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

lastEvaluatedAt.description: The time stamp of the last time that the lifecycle policy was run.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

status.description: The status of the lifecycle policy preview request.

nextToken.description: The nextToken value to include in a future GetLifecyclePolicyPreview request. When the results of a GetLifecyclePolicyPreview request exceed maxResults, this value can be used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

previewResults.description: The results of the lifecycle policy preview request.

summary.description: The list of images that is returned as a result of the action.

imageTags.description: The list of tags associated with this image.

imagePushedAt.description: The date and time, expressed in standard JavaScript date format, at which the current image was pushed to the repository.

action.description: The type of action to be taken.

type.description: The type of action to be taken.

targetStorageClass.description: The target storage class for the action. This is only present when the type is TRANSITION.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

policyText.description: The JSON repository policy text associated with the repository.

uploadId.description: The upload ID for the layer upload. This parameter is passed to further UploadLayerPart and CompleteLayerUpload operations.

partSize.description: The size, in bytes, that Amazon ECR expects future layer part uploads to be.

referrers.description: The list of artifacts associated with the subject image.

nextToken.description: The nextToken value to include in a future ListImageReferrers request. When the results of a ListImageReferrers request exceed maxResults, this value can be used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

mediaType.description: The media type of the artifact manifest.

artifactType.description: A string identifying the type of artifact.

size.description: The size, in bytes, of the artifact.

annotations.description: A map of annotations associated with the artifact.

artifactStatus.description: The status of the artifact. Valid values are ACTIVE, ARCHIVED, or ACTIVATING.

imageIds.description: The list of image IDs for the requested repository.

nextToken.description: The nextToken value to include in a future ListImages request. When the results of a ListImages request exceed maxResults, this value can be used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

Key.description: One part of a key-value pair that make up a tag. A key is a general label that acts like a category for more specific tag values.

Value.description: A value acts as a descriptor within a tag category (key).

name.description: Retrieves the name of the account setting.

value.description: Retrieves the value of the specified account setting.

registryId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID associated with the registry containing the image.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository associated with the image.

imageId.description: An object containing the image tag and image digest associated with an image.

imageManifest.description: The image manifest associated with the image.

imageManifestMediaType.description: The manifest media type of the image.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

imageScanningConfiguration.description: The image scanning configuration setting for the repository.

scanOnPush.description: The setting that determines whether images are scanned after being pushed to a repository. If set to true, images will be scanned after being pushed. If this parameter is not specified, it will default to false and images will not be scanned unless a scan is manually started with the API_StartImageScan API.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

imageTagMutability.description: The image tag mutability setting for the repository.

imageTagMutabilityExclusionFilters.description: The list of filters that specify which image tags are excluded from the repository's image tag mutability setting.

filterType.description: The type of filter to apply for excluding image tags from mutability settings.

filter.description: The filter value used to match image tags for exclusion from mutability settings.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

policyText.description: The JSON policy text for your registry.

replicationConfiguration.description: The contents of the replication configuration for the registry.

rules.description: An array of objects representing the replication destinations and repository filters for a replication configuration.
A list of signing rules. Each rule defines a signing profile and optional repository filters that determine which images are automatically signed. Maximum of 10 rules.

principalArn.description: The ARN of the IAM principal that was added to the pull time update exclusion list.

createdAt.description: The date and time, expressed in standard JavaScript date format, when the exclusion was created.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

policyText.description: The JSON repository policy text applied to the repository.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

imageId.description: An object with identifying information for an image in an Amazon ECR repository.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

status.description: The status of the lifecycle policy preview request.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

imageId.description: An object with identifying information for an image in an Amazon ECR repository.

imageStatus.description: The current status of the image after the call to UpdateImageStorageClass is complete. Valid values are ACTIVE, ARCHIVED, and ACTIVATING.

ecrRepositoryPrefix.description: The Amazon ECR repository prefix associated with the pull through cache rule.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

updatedAt.description: The date and time, in JavaScript date format, when the pull through cache rule was updated.

credentialArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Web Services Secrets Manager secret associated with the pull through cache rule.

customRoleArn.description: The ARN of the IAM role associated with the pull through cache rule.

upstreamRepositoryPrefix.description: The upstream repository prefix associated with the pull through cache rule.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryCreationTemplate.description: The details of the repository creation template associated with the request.

prefix.description: The repository namespace prefix associated with the repository creation template.

description.description: The description associated with the repository creation template.

encryptionConfiguration.description: The encryption configuration associated with the repository creation template.

encryptionType.description:
The encryption type to use.

If you use the KMS encryption type, the contents of the repository will be encrypted using server-side encryption with Key Management Service key stored in KMS. When you use KMS to encrypt your data, you can either use the default Amazon Web Services managed KMS key for Amazon ECR, or specify your own KMS key, which you already created. For more information, see Protecting data using server-side encryption with an KMS key stored in Key Management Service (SSE-KMS) in the Amazon Simple Storage Service Console Developer Guide.

If you use the AES256 encryption type, Amazon ECR uses server-side encryption with Amazon S3-managed encryption keys which encrypts the images in the repository using an AES256 encryption algorithm. For more information, see Protecting data using server-side encryption with Amazon S3-managed encryption keys (SSE-S3) in the Amazon Simple Storage Service Console Developer Guide.

kmsKey.description: If you use the KMS encryption type, specify the KMS key to use for encryption. The full ARN of the KMS key must be specified. The key must exist in the same Region as the repository. If no key is specified, the default Amazon Web Services managed KMS key for Amazon ECR will be used.

resourceTags.description: The metadata to apply to the repository to help you categorize and organize. Each tag consists of a key and an optional value, both of which you define. Tag keys can have a maximum character length of 128 characters, and tag values can have a maximum length of 256 characters.

imageTagMutability.description: The tag mutability setting for the repository. If this parameter is omitted, the default setting of MUTABLE will be used which will allow image tags to be overwritten. If IMMUTABLE is specified, all image tags within the repository will be immutable which will prevent them from being overwritten.

imageTagMutabilityExclusionFilters.description: A list of filters that specify which image tags are excluded from the repository creation template's image tag mutability setting.

repositoryPolicy.description: The repository policy to apply to repositories created using the template. A repository policy is a permissions policy associated with a repository to control access permissions.

lifecyclePolicy.description: The lifecycle policy to use for repositories created using the template.

appliedFor.description: A list of enumerable Strings representing the repository creation scenarios that this template will apply towards. The two supported scenarios are PULL_THROUGH_CACHE and REPLICATION

customRoleArn.description: The ARN of the role to be assumed by Amazon ECR. Amazon ECR will assume your supplied role when the customRoleArn is specified. When this field isn't specified, Amazon ECR will use the service-linked role for the repository creation template.

createdAt.description: The date and time, in JavaScript date format, when the repository creation template was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time, in JavaScript date format, when the repository creation template was last updated.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

repositoryName.description: The repository name associated with the request.

uploadId.description: The upload ID associated with the request.

lastByteReceived.description: The integer value of the last byte received in the request.

ecrRepositoryPrefix.description: The Amazon ECR repository prefix associated with the pull through cache rule.

registryId.description: The registry ID associated with the request.

upstreamRegistryUrl.description: The upstream registry URL associated with the pull through cache rule.

credentialArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Web Services Secrets Manager secret associated with the pull through cache rule.

customRoleArn.description: The ARN of the IAM role associated with the pull through cache rule.

upstreamRepositoryPrefix.description: The upstream repository prefix associated with the pull through cache rule.

isValid.description: Whether or not the pull through cache rule was validated. If true, Amazon ECR was able to reach the upstream registry and authentication was successful. If false, there was an issue and validation failed. The failure reason indicates the cause.

failure.description: The reason the validation failed. For more details about possible causes and how to address them, see Using pull through cache rules in the Amazon Elastic Container Registry User Guide.
